data.title: Rubrik outlines $1.453B subscription ARR target for FY26 amid AI and identity resilience expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Rubrik, Inc. (RBRK) Q3 2026

Management View

  • CEO Bipul Sinha opened the call by stating, "our third quarter results were exceptional. Every quarter for the past several quarters, we have successfully delivered in many cases, record numbers. In fact, this quarter has been our best ever. We have not only added record net new subscription ARR, but also generated tremendous free cash flow. And I'm happy to report we once again exceeded all guided metrics across top line and profitability."
  • Sinha highlighted five key metrics: subscription ARR of $1.35 billion, net new subscription ARR of $94 million, subscription revenue of $336 million, subscription NRR over 120%, and 2,638 customers with $100,000 or more in subscription ARR. He emphasized, "on profitability, we once again made material improvement in subscription ARR contribution margin, up about 1,400 basis points year-over-year."
  • Sinha outlined Rubrik's strategic positioning as "the security and AI operations company," noting increased demand for cyber resilience and the company's ongoing focus on data protection, identity resilience, and AI operations. He stated, "our mission is to lead Rubrik into our next era as the, let me repeat, the security and AI operations company."
  • The CEO announced the launch of Rubrik Agent Cloud, a new product suite for enterprise AI agent management and resilience, and integration with Microsoft Copilot Studio and AWS Bedrock. He added, "Rubrik Agent Cloud is currently in beta, but we are very excited about the early customer enthusiasm."
  • Sinha described rapid momentum in identity resilience, reporting about $20 million in subscription ARR and "we more than doubled the total number of identity customers. In fact, 40% of those identity customers added in the third quarter were net new to Rubrik."
  • CFO Kiran Choudary stated, "We had a very strong Q3 marked by record net new subscription ARR and continued improvement in profitability metrics. These results reinforce our leading position in the large and expanding cyber resilience market. We are raising our Q4 outlook as we look forward to a strong close to the fiscal year."

Outlook

  • Choudary provided guidance: "In Q4, we expect revenue of $341 million to $343 million, up 33% or approximately 30% when normalized for material rights. We expect non-GAAP EPS of negative $0.12 to negative $0.10 based on approximately 201 million weighted average shares outstanding. For the full year fiscal 2026, we now expect subscription ARR in the range of $1.439 billion to $1.453 billion, reflecting a year-over-year growth rate of approximately 32%. We expect total revenue for the full year fiscal 2026 in the range of $1.280 billion to $1.282 billion, up approximately 44% or 35% when normalized for material rights."
  • Choudary also stated, "We expect material rights related to our cloud transformation to contribute approximately $68 million to revenue in fiscal year 2026. We expect non-GAAP subscription ARR contribution margins of approximately 9%. We expect non-GAAP EPS of negative $0.20 to negative $0.16 based on approximately 197 million weighted average shares outstanding for the full year."
  • Management indicated revenue growth may face headwinds in fiscal 2027 due to reduced material rights revenue but highlighted that normalized revenue growth would be ahead of subscription ARR growth.

Financial Results

  • Choudary reported, "subscription ARR of $1.35 billion, growing 34%. We added $94 million in net new subscription ARR, a record amount for Rubrik. We continue to drive adoption of our Rubrik Security Cloud, which resulted in $1.17 billion of cloud ARR up 53%."
  • "For our third quarter, subscription revenue was $336 million, up 52%. Total revenue was $350 million, up 48%. Revenue in Q3 primarily benefited from a strong ARR growth. However, we again had tailwinds from our cloud transformation, resulting in higher nonrecurring revenue, which is accounted for as material rights. Material rights contributed approximately $25 million to revenue this quarter, modestly ahead of our expectation."
  • "Our non-GAAP gross margin was 83% in the third quarter, compared to 79% in the year ago period. Our gross margin benefited from the revenue outperformance, including higher nonrecurring revenue and the improved efficiency of our customer support organization."
  • Free cash flow was reported as $77 million for the quarter, compared to $16 million the prior year period.

Q&A

  • Saket Kalia, Barclays: Asked about the additive nature of identity solutions to deals. Sinha responded, "Identity is completely additive to the deal. So it is a net new buyer for Rubrik... In fact, this product went into GA -- first GA in December last year. And we are seeing 40% of new customers last quarter is net new to Rubrik."
  • Matthew Martino, Goldman Sachs: Inquired about drivers of accelerated legacy replacement bookings. Sinha stated, "It's both. It's definitely preparing for AI and modernizing the infrastructure, that's a big driver. And the other driver is cyber attacks are inevitable and Rubrik has a unique platform that delivers cyber recovery."
  • Fatima Boolani, Citi: Asked about the impact of platform expansion on sales strategy. Sinha replied, "Our strategy has always been give customer a comprehensive platform and be the strategic IT vendor for that customer for years and years to come."
  • John DiFucci, Guggenheim: Sought clarity on Rubrik's positioning in AI agent security. Sinha explained, "Rubrik is squarely at the intersection of operations and security. So this is a natural place for us to deliver a complete agentic operation platform."
  • Multiple analysts probed on the durability of net retention rates, monetization of new product suites, and go-to-market dynamics for AI and identity.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed positive sentiment, frequently congratulating management and highlighting strong execution, but also posed probing questions on product monetization, competitive positioning, and new market opportunities.
  • Management maintained a confident and forward-looking tone in prepared remarks, using phrases like "we are very excited about what's ahead for identity resilience" and "we are confident in closing out this year strong and continuing our great momentum into the next year and beyond." In the Q&A, management maintained composure and provided comprehensive responses.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analyst and management sentiment remained strongly positive, with increased excitement around identity resilience and AI agent operations expansion.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Subscription ARR grew from over $1.25 billion in Q2 to $1.35 billion in Q3. Net new subscription ARR increased from $71 million to $94 million quarter-over-quarter.
  • Subscription revenue and total revenue both increased sequentially, with subscription revenue rising from $297 million to $336 million, and total revenue from $310 million to $350 million.
  • The guidance for full-year subscription ARR increased to a range of $1.439 billion to $1.453 billion from the prior quarter's $1.408 billion to $1.416 billion.
  • Management tone remained confident, with a greater emphasis this quarter on the momentum in identity resilience and the launch of Rubrik Agent Cloud. Analyst focus shifted more toward monetization and go-to-market strategies for new product lines and the durability of high net retention rates.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted "dynamics related to our successful cloud transformation and a substantial reduction in material rights revenue will represent a headwind to our reported revenue growth" in fiscal 2027.
  • There is early-stage risk in scaling Rubrik Agent Cloud, which is currently in beta. Sinha stated, "We are still in the initial phase of a multiyear effort to scale Rubrik's AI solutions."
  • Analysts queried about the volatility of net new ARR and the timing for general availability of new product suites, highlighting risks around pacing and adoption of new offerings.

Final Takeaway

Rubrik’s Q3 2026 call emphasized record-setting ARR and free cash flow, a raised full-year outlook, and continued strong customer expansion in both identity resilience and AI operations. Management highlighted robust demand and acceleration in legacy replacement, as well as successful early adoption of new product suites such as Rubrik Agent Cloud and Identity Resilience. Guidance reflects confidence in sustaining growth, even as the company navigates accounting headwinds associated with its cloud transformation. The company remains focused on innovation across cyber resilience and AI, underpinned by disciplined execution and strategic investments in R&D and go-to-market capabilities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Asia markets subdued trading ahead of crucial Fed rate decision, follow RBI's anticipated rate cut
data.description:
Asia stock markets were mixed on Friday following a muted Wall Street session overnight, with markets preparing for next week’s Fed interest rate decision, while the region also digested the expected interest rate reduction announced by the Reserve Bank of India.

Gold prices fell below $4,200 per ounce on Friday as investors awaited key US data ahead of next week’s FOMC decision.

Retail sales in Singapore rose by 4.5% year-on-year in October 2025, accelerating from a 2.8% gain in the previous month. 

South Korea's current account surplus shrank to $6.81 billion in October 2025 from $13.47 billion in September, with the decline driven by the Chuseok holiday and higher US tariffs. 

Japan (NKY:IND) fell 1.18% to below 50,500, while the broader Topix Index fell 1% to 3,365 on Friday, trimming gains from the previous session. The Japanese yen firmed around 155 per dollar on Friday and was on track for a second consecutive weekly advance.

Rising global bond yields also pressured equities, with the Bank of Japan signaling a potential rate hike later this month. 

Household spending in Japan fell 3.0% y/y in October 2025, missing market expectations for a 1.0% rise and reversing a 1.8% gain in the prior month.

Japan’s coincident economic index, which tracks key indicators including factory output, employment, and retail sales, rose to 115.4 in October 2025 from an upwardly revised 114.9 in the previous month.

Japan’s foreign reserves rose by $11.98 billion to $1.36 trillion in November 2025, the highest level since March 2022.

Japan’s leading economic index, which gauges the outlook for the coming months based on indicators such as job offers and consumer sentiment, rose to 110.0 in October 2025 from a downwardly revised 108.2 in September.

China (SHCOMP) rose 0.46% to around 3,870, while the Shenzhen Component dropped 0.15% to 12,985 on Friday, with mainland stocks set to end the week lower as investors awaited policy signals from high-level meetings this month.

Sentiment weakened as traders turned cautious ahead of key November data from China next week, including CPI, PPI, and trade figures, while also awaiting potential policy signals from the upcoming Central Economic Work Conference. 

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.17% to 25,855 in Friday’s morning session, reversing gains from the prior day amid broad sector losses.

India (SENSEX) rose 0.36% to 85,569 in morning trade on Friday, extending gains from the previous session following the RBI’s 25-bps cut in key repo rates and revised GDP and inflation forecasts.

The Reserve Bank of India lowered its key repo rate by 25 bps to 5.25% during its December 2025 meeting and maintained a neutral stance, in line with market expectations. 

The central bank raised its GDP growth forecast for FY2025/26 to 7.3%, up from a previous estimate of 6.8%, while revising headline inflation down to 2.0% from 2.6%, keeping it well within the 2%–6% target range.

Australia (AS51) rose 0.21% opened largely unchanged at around 8,620 on Friday, halting its three-session winning streak, and headed for just a 0.1% weekly gain as investors assessed the Reserve Bank of Australia’s monetary policy outlook. 

The central bank is widely expected to keep rates steady at its policy meeting next week and will likely hold early next year.

In the U.S. on Thursday, all three major indexes essentially flat as investors focused on next week’s Federal Reserve decision and parsed mixed US labor signals that left markets heavily pricing a 25bp cut. 

Focus is on the postponed September PCE report, the Fed’s preferred inflation gauge, due later today.

U.S. stock futures held steady on Friday as investors looked ahead to a key inflation report that could shape the Federal Reserve’s policy outlook: Dow +0.03%; S&P 500 +0.18%; Nasdaq +0.35%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: Protara Therapeutics slumps 12%, raises $75M equity at $5.75 per share
data.description:
• Protara Therapeutics (TARA) priced its underwritten public offering of ~13.04M shares at a price to the public of $5.75 per share.
  • The underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional ~1.96M shares. All shares in the offering are being sold by Protara.
  • Gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be ~$75M.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 8, 2025.
  • Net proceeds will be used to fund the clinical development of TARA-002, as well as the development of other clinical programs and remaining for working capital and other general corporate purposes.
  • The stock price slumped about 12% on Thursday during after-market hours of trading.

data.title: OpenAI and NextDC to build $4.6 billion data center in Sydney
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) and NextDC (OTCPK:NXDCF) have signed an MOU to jointly develop a A$7 billion ($4.6 billion) hyperscale AI campus and large-scale GPU supercluster at NextDC's S7 site in Sydney's Eastern Creek precinct.

The facility, located 45 kilometers west of Sydney's center, offers up to 550 megawatts of potential capacity and represents OpenAI's (OPENAI) first major AI infrastructure push in Australia.

The two firms will collaborate on planning, development, and operation of the AI infrastructure partnership in western Sydney. The first phase of the S7 project is expected to be delivered in H2 2027, subject to approvals, the company said in a statement. 

Brisbane-based NextDC's stock price surged about 10% on the Australian exchange after the announcement, pushing its market value above A$9 billion.

data.title: Ulta Beauty in charts: Q3 reports double-digit sales growth driven by 6.3% rise in comparable sales and net new store contribution
DocuSign in charts: Subscription revenue maintains single-digit growth pace in Q3; Billings up 10.3% Y/Y
Netflix puts up top bid for Warner Bros Discovery - reports
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) emerged as the highest bidder for Warner Bros. Discovery's (WBD), according to a Reuters report that cites sources familiar with the matter.

Another Bloomberg report also suggested that Netflix (NFLX) had entered exclusive talks to buy WBD's (WBD) studio and streaming service and is offering a $5 billion breakup fee if regulators don't approve the deal. The report also indicated that the deal could be announced in five days.

Netflix and Warner Bros. Discovery did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

Previous day, a CNBC report suggested the same, with the Netflix offer consisting of 85% cash.

The acquisition would consolidate Netflix's (NFLX) dominance in streaming by absorbing Warner Bros. (WBD) studios and HBO Max, potentially creating bundled offerings to cut consumer costs. WBD (WBD) initiated the sale process in October amid financial pressures.

data.title: Hewlett Packard in charts: Networking revenue sees highest Y/Y rise of 150.2% in FQ4
Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
08:30 AM Personal Income and Outlays 
 
Personal income represents the income that households receive from all sources, including wages and salaries, fringe benefits such as employer contributions to private pension plans, proprietors' income, income from rent, dividends, and interest, and transfer payments. 
 
Income and spending are both expected to be up a robust 0.4 percent on the month. PCE prices are seen up 0.3 percent on the month for total and core.
 
10:00 AM Consumer Sentiment 
 
The University of Michigan's Consumer Survey Center questions households each month on their assessment of current conditions and expectations of future conditions.
 
Not a pretty picture for consumer sentiment, with 52.0 expected for the first read on December versus an abysmal 51.0 in the November final.
10:00 AM Factory Orders 
 
Factory orders represent the dollar level of new orders for both durable and nondurable goods. This report gives more complete information than the advance durable goods report, which is released one or two weeks earlier in the month.
1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count 
 
The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs.
 
3:00 PM Consumer Credit 
 
The dollar value of consumer installment credit outstanding. Changes in consumer credit indicate the state of consumer finances and portend future spending patterns. 
 
A trendlike rise of $9.4 billion is the call for October.

data.title: WEC Energy raises dividend by 7% to $0.9525
data.description:
• WEC Energy (WEC) declares $0.9525/share quarterly dividend, 6.7% increase from prior dividend of $0.8925.
  • Forward yield 3.58%
  • Payable March 1; for shareholders of record Feb. 13; ex-div Feb. 13.
  • See WEC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
